manufacturerGfciInstruction: Typical two-pole circuit breaker without GFCI; all wiring must comply with local codes.

sources.title: Select Your Controller and Steam Head
sources.quote: Recommended ceiling height is 8 feet or less. For each foot above 8 feet, you must upgrade to the next steam generator size.

sources.title: Select Your Controller and Steam Head
sources.quote: The steam generator needs to be installed within 25 feet of the steam head. A separate, dedicated 240 V circuit is required. No GFCI should be connected to this circuit.

sources.title: Steam Generator (5 kW - 11 kW) Installation Instructions
sources.quote: Recommended ceiling height is 8' (2.4 m) or less. Maximum ceiling height is 10' (3 m).

sources.title: Steam Generator (13 kW - 15 kW) Installation Instructions
sources.quote: A minimum air gap of 6 inches (152 mm) must be provided around the steam generator on at least three sides.

sources.title: Steam Generator (5 kW - 11 kW) Installation Instructions
sources.quote: Position the steam head(s) 6 inches (152 mm) above the shower floor. Position the steam control user interface 60 inches (1524 mm) above the shower floor.

assumptions: Current KOHLER sources do not explicitly define fractional-foot rounding. This planner conservatively rounds each started foot above 8 feet upward and flags partial feet for manufacturer review.
Current KOHLER sources do not publish a surface-material sizing multiplier, so finish does not alter generator sizing.
The no-GFCI statement is displayed only as a manufacturer instruction and must be reconciled with current local code by a licensed electrician.
